title: 4  Projection Error
text:
# remotes::install_github("hypertidy/geographiclib")
library(geographiclib)
library(data.table)
library(sf)

Linking to GEOS 3.14.1, GDAL 3.12.2, PROJ 9.7.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE

library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)
sb <- qs2::qs_read("../data/RAW/hud_detects.qs2") |>
  as.data.table() |>
  unique(by = c("long", "lat")) |>
  _[, .(lon = long, lat = lat)]
atl <- fread("../data/RAW/fixed_rounded_station_locations.csv") |>
  unique(by = c("r.lon", "r.lat")) |>
  _[, .(lon = r.lon, lat = r.lat)]
st <- fread("../data/RAW/sandtigers.csv") |>
  unique(by = c("Longitude", "Latitude")) |>
  _[, .(lon = Longitude, lat = Latitude)]

locations <- list(sb = sb, atl = atl, st = st)


truth <- lapply(locations, geodesic_distance_matrix)
centers <- lapply(
  locations,
  \(.) {
    .[, .(med_lon = median(lon, na.rm = T), med_lat = median(lat, na.rm = T))]
  }
)


proj_dist <- function(stations, centers) {
  list(
    aeqd = azeq_fwd(stations, centers[[1]], centers[[2]]) |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix(),
    utm_auto = utmups_fwd(stations)[, 1:2] |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix(),
    utm17 = tm_exact_fwd(stations, -81) |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix(),
    utm18 = tm_exact_fwd(stations, -75) |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix(),
    utm19 = tm_exact_fwd(stations, -69) |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix(),
    tm = tm_exact_fwd(stations, centers[[1]]) |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix(),
    cass = cassini_fwd(stations, centers[[1]], centers[[2]])[, 1:2] |>
      dist() |>
      as.matrix()
  )
}

projected_distances <- mapply(proj_dist, locations, centers, SIMPLIFY = F)


proj_errs <- function(dists, truth, species) {
  lapply(dists[[species]], \(.) {
    (. - truth[[species]])[lower.tri(truth[[species]])]
  })
}


errors <- sapply(
  names(projected_distances),
  \(.) proj_errs(projected_distances, truth, species = .),
  simplify = FALSE,
  USE.NAMES = TRUE
) |>
  lapply(\(.) as.data.table(.) |> melt()) |>
  rbindlist(idcol = 'species')

Warning in melt.data.table(as.data.table(.)): id.vars and measure.vars are
internally guessed when both are 'NULL'. All non-numeric/integer/logical type
columns are considered id.vars, which in this case are columns []. Consider
providing at least one of 'id' or 'measure' vars in future.
Warning in melt.data.table(as.data.table(.)): id.vars and measure.vars are
internally guessed when both are 'NULL'. All non-numeric/integer/logical type
columns are considered id.vars, which in this case are columns []. Consider
providing at least one of 'id' or 'measure' vars in future.
Warning in melt.data.table(as.data.table(.)): id.vars and measure.vars are
internally guessed when both are 'NULL'. All non-numeric/integer/logical type
columns are considered id.vars, which in this case are columns []. Consider
providing at least one of 'id' or 'measure' vars in future.



ggplot() +
  geom_histogram(
    data = errors[variable != "utm_auto"],
    aes(x = abs(value), fill = variable),
    position = 'dodge',
    binwidth = 50
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 500) +
  facet_wrap(~species, scales = "free", ncol = 1)

Warning: Removed 3288 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
(`stat_bin()`).

title: Flyway Assessment of Offshore Wind Farm Impacts on Migratory Fishes
2  Geodesic Regression
text:
Explain geoid

Imagine circle through the center of the Earth with the Earth’s radius
Line from N pole to S pole through Greenwich, along the 0* meridian
Continue from S pole to N pole along the 180* meridian
Can transform this by changing the meridian (longitude), changing the poles (latitude), or both

Explain geodesic

Line along the surface of this circle

title: 2  Geodesic Regression
text:
2.2 Geodesic regression

Even here, we are projecting into Euclidean space to make math simpler
Rienmannian manifolds – a way to express curvature

title: 2  Geodesic Regression
section: 2.3 As it applies to ROSA project
text:
2.3 As it applies to ROSA project

2.3.1 Geodesic measure

Convert lat/lon coordinates to unit sphere (S2 library)

Assumptions and innacuracies here, however:

spheres are well-defined Reinmanninan manifolds
I have not found geoids (WGS84/GRS80) defined as Reinmanninan manifolds. I am unsure if this is because people have already tried it and it’s a dead end or if the math is just new.


Conduct geodesic regression to find best-fit geodesic through points



2.3.2 Comparisons

Compare this line against least-squares fit in common “projections”: unprojected, Mercator, UTM (17-19), AEQD, gnomonic
